title: Ultrafiltration and Purification of Conditioned Media Using Pall Jumbsosep and Izon qEV-10
author: Joshua A Welsh
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.beqnjdve

steps.title: Make up 70% ethanol with HPLC or other pure water
steps.instruction: Make up 70% ethanol with HPLC or other pure water

steps.title: Filter at least 20 mL of...
steps.instruction: Filter at least 20 mL of the 70% ethanol with a 0.02 µm syringe-driven filter, or other small pore size filter

steps.title: Obtain a Jumbosep sample reservoir, a...
steps.instruction: Obtain a Jumbosep sample reservoir, a Jumbsosep filtrate receiver, and a Jumbosep insert

steps.title: Snap the reservoir onto the receiver
steps.instruction: Snap the reservoir onto the receiver

steps.title: Without contacting the inside of the...
steps.instruction: Without contacting the inside of the reservoir or the insert, place the insert into the reservoir

steps.title: Use the blunt end of an...
steps.instruction: Use the blunt end of an aspirating tip to firmly push the insert into place

steps.title: Fill the reservoir with at least...
steps.instruction: Fill the reservoir with at least 10 mL of the prepared filtered 70% ethanol

steps.title: Swirl the device around to maximize...
steps.instruction: Swirl the device around to maximize ethanol contact and let sit for a 5 minutes

steps.title: Spin the device (with an appropriate...
steps.instruction: Spin the device (with an appropriate swinging bucket rotor and adaptor insert, along with another balancing device, as needed) at 2500 RCF for 5 minutes to concentrate ethanol down to the device's dead volume of 5 mL

steps.title: To separate the assembled device, remove...
steps.instruction: To separate the assembled device, remove the reservoir cap, clasp hands behind the device, place both thumbs on the lip of the sample reservoir, and press upward and backward.

steps.title: Flush the reservoir with ~10 mL...
steps.instruction: Flush the reservoir with ~10 mL of sterile PBS and aspirate all volume from the receiver

steps.title: Assemble the device, add 60 mL...
steps.instruction: Assemble the device, add 60 mL of sterile PBS to the reservoir and spin at 3000 RCF for 5 minutes

steps.title: Aspirate all volume from the receiver,...
steps.instruction: Aspirate all volume from the receiver, and the device is ready to have conditioned media loaded onto it

steps.title: Place the reservoir cap onto the reservoir
steps.instruction: Place the reservoir cap onto the reservoir

steps.title: Use a tissue to blot any...
steps.instruction: Use a tissue to blot any fluid which may be present on rim of reservoir and receiver

steps.title: Cell culture media is ready to...
steps.instruction: Cell culture media is ready to be harvested from cells when they are 50% to 80% confluent, typically 48 hours after cells have been seeded into phenol-red-free, bovine EV-depleted complete media

steps.title: Using a 50 mL serological pipet...
steps.instruction: Using a 50 mL serological pipet tip, transfer the complete volume of media in each flask of cells to a sterile 50 mL conical tube

steps.title: Spin the tubes at 500 RCF for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Spin the tubes at 500 RCF for 10 minutes

steps.title: Transfer the supernatants from the 50...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatants from the 50 mL conical tubes to a new set 50 mL conical tubes, leaving behind a small but visible pellet of cells and cell debris

steps.title: Spin the tubes at 2500 RCF for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Spin the tubes at 2500 RCF for 10 minutes

steps.title: Transfer the supernatants from the 50...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatants from the 50 mL conical tubes (leaving behind 1 to 2 mL of fluid at the bottom of the tubes) to a sterile media storage bottle for temporary storage during the subsequent ultrafiltration process

steps.title: Wipe down with 70% ethanol two...
steps.instruction: Wipe down with 70% ethanol two 750 mL centrifuge bucket inserts and the insides of the 750 mL centrifuge buckets

steps.title: Load conditioned media into the reservoir...
steps.instruction: Load conditioned media into the reservoir until the volume reaches the 60 mL mark

steps.title: Spin the device at 3000 RCF...
steps.instruction: Spin the device at 3000 RCF for ~20 minutes checking to ensure only the ~5 mL dead volume of fluid remains in the sample reservoir after the spin

steps.title: Disasemble the device, aspirate the filtrate,...
steps.instruction: Disasemble the device, aspirate the filtrate, and reasemble the device before addition of conditioned media

steps.title: Add conditioned media to the sample...
steps.instruction: Add conditioned media to the sample reservoir until it reaches the 60 mL mark

steps.title: If you are trying to balance...
steps.instruction: If you are trying to balance two devices but have less than 60 mL of media for each device, PBS can be added to one of the devices to ensure the volumes are equal

steps.title: Spin at 3000 RCF for 30...
steps.instruction: Spin at 3000 RCF for 30 minutes at room temperature or until only ~5 mL of fluid remains in the sample reservoir

steps.title: Continue the process of adding additional...
steps.instruction: Continue the process of adding additional cleared conditioned media until all media has been transferred to the sample reservoir, increasing the centrifuge time as necessary (typically 5 to 10 minutes more per additional spin)

steps.title: If the last volume of media...
steps.instruction: If the last volume of media remaining to be filtered is not 60 mL, PBS can be added to the reservoir to fill it to the 60 mL marker

steps.title: After the last spin with added...
steps.instruction: After the last spin with added cleared media, fill the sample reservoir with 60 mL of PBS, ensuring resuspension of any material adhered to the bottom of the reservoir and insert, and complete the final 3000 RCF spin at RT, so that there is a ~5 mL dead volume This is a wash step to remove any soluble protein <100 kDa that may be remaining in the 5 mL dead volume.

steps.title: Being careful not to spill concentrate...
steps.instruction: Being careful not to spill concentrate or create excessive bubbles, use a P1000 micropipette to thoroughly resuspend the concentrate which is sitting on top of the insert in the sample reservoir

steps.title: Transfer the ~5 mL from the...
steps.instruction: Transfer the ~5 mL from the top of the sample reservoir to Eppendorf Low-protein binding 5.0 mL tubes, placing a maximum volume of 4 mL in each tube to prevent overflow

steps.title: Label and store the conditioned media...
steps.instruction: Label and store the conditioned media concentrate at 4 ˚C overnight, do not store for more than a short period of time without purifying the concentrate

steps.title: Remove a new qEV-10 column from...
steps.instruction: Remove a new qEV-10 column from its 4˚C storage location at the start of work day to allow to warm to room temperature

steps.title: Use a ring stand and metal...
steps.instruction: Use a ring stand and metal clamp to position the column vertically

steps.title: Rinse 5 mL of PBS from...
steps.instruction: Rinse 5 mL of PBS from a new, unopened, room temperature bottle of PBS through the loading chamber into a waste container

steps.title: Unscrew the top cap from the column
steps.instruction: Unscrew the top cap from the column

steps.title: Fill the column with PBS until it fills the column
steps.instruction: Fill the column with PBS until it fills the column

steps.title: Press the loading chamber into the...
steps.instruction: Press the loading chamber into the top of the column, ensuring a tight seal between the two units

steps.title: Fill the loading chamber to the top with PBS
steps.instruction: Fill the loading chamber to the top with PBS

steps.title: Unscrew the bottom cap from the...
steps.instruction: Unscrew the bottom cap from the column, PBS should flow through the column and into a waste container

steps.title: Once around half the PBS volume...
steps.instruction: Once around half the PBS volume in the loading chamber has flushed through the column, fill it back up to the top with PBS

steps.title: While the column is being flushed,...
steps.instruction: While the column is being flushed, label 14, 5mL low protein-binding Eppendorf tubes with the a fraction number (1 through 14) and date. A marker can be used to highlight the 5.0 mL volume graduation on the tube

steps.title: As soon as all of the...
steps.instruction: As soon as all of the loaded PBS has moved through the loading chamber, position the "Fraction 1" 5.0 mL tube under the colum. Gently vortex and invert the concentrated media and add it to the loading chamber 1 mL at a time, ensuring to pipette the media directly onto the reservoir filter and not the walls of the chamber. The time elapsed from the last drop of PBS eluting to loading the cell culture media concentrate onto the column should be as short as possible.

steps.title: As soon as all of the...
steps.instruction: As soon as all of the loaded concentrated media has passed through the loading chamber, the loading reservoir can be filled to the top with PBS

steps.title: Continue collecting fractions in 5
steps.instruction: Continue collecting fractions in 5.0 mL increments until all fractions have been collected, refilling the loading chamber to the top with PBS when the PBS level falls by about half

steps.title: While PBS continues to flush through...
steps.instruction: While PBS continues to flush through column, into a waste container, collected fraction tube lids can be closed and set aside

steps.title: Once the entire volume of PBS...
steps.instruction: Once the entire volume of PBS in the loading chamber has run through, add 5 mL of filtered 0.5 M NaOH to the chamber and allow it to pass through the column. The NaOH can be added by pipetting along the walls of the reservoir to wash away any contaminants

steps.title: Fill the buffer reservoir to the...
steps.instruction: Fill the buffer reservoir to the top with PBS and allow it all to pass through the column except for the last ~1 mL to prevent drying the column

steps.title: Refill the buffer reservoir to the top with PBS
steps.instruction: Refill the buffer reservoir to the top with PBS

steps.title: After the majority of the buffer...
steps.instruction: After the majority of the buffer has eluted use a pH test strip to ensure the eluting buffer is neutral

steps.title: Fill the column up with 5...
steps.instruction: Fill the column up with 5 mL of filtered 20% EtOH and allow it to flush through column

steps.title: Place the bottom cap back on the column
steps.instruction: Place the bottom cap back on the column

steps.title: Pipette filtered 20% EtOH into the...
steps.instruction: Pipette filtered 20% EtOH into the top of the column until it is filled to the top with EtOH.

steps.title: Place the top cap back on the column
steps.instruction: Place the top cap back on the column

steps.title: Allow the loading chamber to dry in the hood overnight
steps.instruction: Allow the loading chamber to dry in the hood overnight

steps.title: Return all column components to the...
steps.instruction: Return all column components to the box they came from and lable the box with date used, cell line used on, and volume of concentrated media loaded into the column.
